svg.text: BIBLICAL HEBREW
HISTORY — SEMITIC SISTER LANGUAGES
PROTO-SEMITIC ORIGIN
Hebrew and Arabic both descended from Proto-Semitic (~3500 BCE)
Proto-Semitic homeland: probably northern Arabian Peninsula or Levant
Semitic family: Akkadian, Ugaritic, Phoenician, Aramaic, Hebrew, Arabic...
Hebrew diverged by ~1200 BCE; Classical Arabic stabilized ~600 CE
BIBLICAL HEBREW TIMELINE
Archaic Hebrew: Song of Deborah (~1100 BCE), oldest biblical poetry
Classical Biblical: Torah, Prophets, Writings (~900-400 BCE)
Dead Sea Scrolls (~200 BCE-70 CE): oldest surviving biblical manuscripts
Masoretes fixed text (~600-900 CE): Aleppo Codex (925 CE) = standard
HEBREW: A LIVING DEAD LANGUAGE
Hebrew "died" as vernacular ~200 CE; survived in sacred use only
Ben-Yehuda began revival 1881; modern Hebrew declared 1948
Biblical Hebrew still read liturgically worldwide every week
ARABIC HISTORY · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
CLASSICAL ARABIC AND THE QURAN
Pre-Islamic: oral poetry (Muallaqat, ~6th century CE)
Quran (~610-632 CE): fixed Classical Arabic as sacred standard
Quran: never translated in liturgy (Arabic IS the sacred form)
Islamic Golden Age (~750-1258 CE): Arabic = language of science + math
ARABIC SPREAD AND MODERN FORMS
Arabic spread from Iberia to Indonesia with Islam (7th-15th c.)
MSA (Modern Standard Arabic): written lingua franca; no L1 speakers
Dialects: Egyptian, Levantine, Gulf, Maghrebi (often mutually opaque)
~300-400M speakers; 22 countries; UN official language
HEBREW AND ARABIC: SHARED ROOTS
Shalom (Hebrew) — Salaam (Arabic): same S-L-M root, "peace"
Elohim (Hebrew God) — Allah (Arabic): same El/Il root, "God"
Adam (Hebrew, Arabic): same word, same story
Two Semitic literatures: Torah and Quran — sister scriptures
Geographically intertwined for 3000 years; linguistically never apart
PLATE 09d ALT · BIBLICAL HEBREW / ARABIC · HISTORY
